appraisal_events.evidence: « verve d'insincérité », « intonations menteuses », « ton factice »; puis, brusque revirement: « Je crois que j'ai trouvé le moyen de me faire inviter… »; enfin: « Swann ne fut pas invité. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator unmasks Swann’s invectives against the Verdurins as an insincere screen for his jealousy and his desire to be reinvited to be with Odette; he tries to obtain an invitation and fails.

status_effects.explanation: His outburst appears fabricated and self-interested, which diminishes him in the narrator’s eyes.
Locally, Swann is excluded from the dinner at Chatou despite his attempt to get himself invited.
